items.title: Tencent released the Hy3 open model
items.oneliner: Tencent's 295B-parameter Hy3 activates 21B and claims 2x to 5x gains, raising the floor for open MoE models.

items.title: Meta launched the Muse Image model
items.oneliner: Meta's Muse Image debuts near the top of image leaderboards, making strong multimodal editing harder to treat as niche.

items.title: The AI Scientist reached Nature
items.oneliner: Nature published an AI system that ideates, codes, runs experiments, and drafts papers, pushing autonomy from demos toward science.

items.title: GitHub's AI agent leaked private repositories
items.oneliner: Researchers tricked GitHub's AI agent into exposing private repos, which should reset trust assumptions around agent permissions.

items.title: Dialogflow exposed enterprise chatbot pipelines
items.oneliner: Varonis says one edit permission in Dialogflow CX could hijack chatbot code paths, turning low-level access into full compromise.

items.title: Verified GitHub commits were not unique
items.oneliner: New research says GitHub can accept multiple valid signatures for equivalent commits, exposing a subtle software supply-chain weakness.

items.title: Illinois passed a frontier AI law
items.oneliner: Illinois now requires major AI developers to disclose safety practices and report incidents, with independent audits starting in 2027.

items.title: China may curb overseas model access
items.oneliner: Beijing is considering limits on overseas access to top Chinese models, threatening the global supply of cheap frontier inference.

items.title: Anthropic locked in long-term data center capacity
items.oneliner: Anthropic's 20-year TeraWulf lease implies roughly $19B in contracted revenue, showing compute access is becoming infrastructure policy.

items.title: DeepSeek is building its own chip
items.oneliner: DeepSeek is reportedly developing an in-house AI chip, signaling that model labs now want silicon leverage, not just GPU allocations.
